content_of_post_to_comment: Motivation as a concept is a myth that can make us feel broken or inadequate when we are just tired, scared, or aimless. You do not need to feel ready to start. You do not need to feel confident to do what needs to be done. You can do it while your hands are shaking and your brain is screaming at you to quit. Action does not follow motivation. Motivation follows action. The moment you take the first step (well, sometimes two or three) fear loses its power. Stop waiting for the perfect fearless version of yourself to show up and let your messy tired version start today.
comment: The bit about not waiting for the confident version of yourself hit home. One thing I'd add is that sometimes people label everything as lack of motivation when they're actually exhausted. Tiny actions still matter, but so does recognizing when the problem is depleted energy rather than fear or avoidance.
